idea.hook: Our Solar System is HUGE, right? Think again.
facts: The Oort Cloud, our Solar System's true edge, spans 2 light-years across.
That's still less than half the distance to Proxima Centauri, our nearest star, 4.2 light-years away!
Now imagine the 'small' Orion Nebula. It's 24 light-years wide – enough space for *six* distances to Proxima Centauri end-to-end!
idea.payoff: Yet, this entire nebula would be just a tiny speck within our 100,000 light-year wide Milky Way galaxy!
idea.title: 🤯 Our Solar System is TINY! (Cosmic Scale)

scenes.text: You think our Solar System is HUGE?
scenes.visual: A dynamic shot zooming out from Earth to encompass the entire Solar System with its planets and orbits. Stars twinkle in the background.
A conceptual diagram showing the Solar System as a tiny dot at the center, enveloped by a vast, spherical, icy 'Oort Cloud' labeled with '2 Light-Years' across its diameter.

scenes.text: Proxima Centauri: 4.2 LY. Oort Cloud < HALF this!
scenes.visual: Two parallel lines representing distances. One shorter, labeled 'Oort Cloud (2 LY)', and a second, longer line next to it, labeled 'Proxima Centauri (4.2 LY)', visually illustrating the comparison.

scenes.text: Orion Nebula: 24 LY wide. SIX Proxima Centauri lengths!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking image of the Orion Nebula. Overlayed are six small, stacked bars, each representing the 'Proxima Centauri distance' to show scale within the nebula.

scenes.text: Orion Nebula? A TINY speck in our 100,000 LY Milky Way!
scenes.visual: A majestic, rotating full view of the spiral Milky Way galaxy. A single, almost imperceptible pixel or small dot blinks faintly in one of its arms, labeled 'Orion Nebula', emphasizing its minuscule size in the grand scale.

youtube.title: 🤯 Our Solar System is TINY! (Cosmic Scale)
